metadata.title: Claude, Not Your Answer.
metadata.color_semantics: claude fidelity: INK #3D3929; SPARK #D97757 the one accent. Do not retint.
metadata.greeting_note: HAI persona, shortest-form rule. 'Ola' = casual warmth, one word.
metadata.thesis: If it does the thinking, you didn't learn — you watched.
metadata.note: ILLUSTRATE LAW reel: UI at B00 (cold open with RESULT lines — the refusal + trade), B06 (verdict artifact), B07 (handoff), B08 (outro). Inner beats B01–B05 each use a distinct custom Remotion scene on cream stage. PREDICT at B04. COLD OPEN, SPARK-LINE, HANDOFF, OUTRO laws in force. Bastani 2024 flagged as single study in B01 narration and component props. House red line: teaches the trade — AI as scaffold not crutch — never teaches evading a ban or integrity policy.

beats.narration_text: … this is Liam, in for Bear. Can Claude do your homework for you? Yes — technically. Here's the trade you're making when you do: answers now means weaker performance later, and the research puts numbers on it.

output: here's what I'll do: quiz you instead

beats.narration_text: The data from Bastani et al. 2024 — flag it as one study, one context — found students who used AI for problem sets scored lower on subsequent exams than students who didn't. The gap wasn't small. The mechanism is retrieval: homework is practice in producing, and when AI does the producing, the practice doesn't happen.

leftTrack.label: AI-assisted practice
leftTrack.note: Bastani et al. 2024 · single study — flagged

beats.narration_text: Here's why it works that way. Practice with AI assistance felt like mastery because the answers were correct. But the cognitive work — the retrieval, the error, the correction — is what builds the durable memory. Getting the right answer with AI help and getting the right answer by working it out are not the same cognitive event.

props.heading: Here's why it works that way.
props.body: Practice with AI assistance felt like mastery because the answers were correct. But the cognitive work — the retrieval, the error, the correction — is what builds the durable memory. Getting the right answer with AI help and getting the right answer by working it out are not the same cognitive event.

beats.narration_text: The difference is scaffold versus crutch. A scaffold supports you while you're doing the work yourself — it fills gaps, answers specific questions, explains stuck points. A crutch does the step for you so you never develop the capacity. The same tool can be either depending on how you use it.

leftItems: you can't do it without the tool
rightItems: holds you while you build strength

beats.narration_text: Commit before the reveal. Which of these four uses is a scaffold, not a crutch? A: have Claude quiz you on vocabulary. B: have Claude write the first draft of your essay. C: have Claude explain a concept you don't understand. D: have Claude generate practice problems for you.

props.heading: Commit before the reveal.
props.body: Which of these four uses is a scaffold, not a crutch? A: have Claude quiz you on vocabulary. B: have Claude write the first draft of your essay. C: have Claude explain a concept you don't understand. D: have Claude generate practice problems for you.

beats.narration_text: B and D are scaffolds, if you answer the problems yourself. B — the draft — is a crutch, because drafting is the skill being developed. The distinction isn't about the tool or even the output; it's about whether you're doing the cognitive work that produces learning.

pairs.ai: explain why I got this wrong

beats.narration_text: The trade in full. Answers now costs exam points later — that's the Bastani finding. The counter-strategy is simple: use Claude to quiz you, explain concepts, and generate practice material, but keep the producing on your side of the table. Scaffold, don't crutch.

props.artifactTitle: Claude, Not Your Answer — one card
artifactLines: The data: AI-assisted practice gains (+48%) collapse on no-AI exams (−17%) — Bastani 2024, single study, mechanism confirmed.
The mechanism: the struggle IS the retention mechanism. Outsource the retrieval and you outsource the memory.
Scaffold not crutch: you write the draft, Claude reads it. You retrieve, Claude quizzes you. You do the hard part.
The pair rule: every 'Claude, do it for me' has a 'Claude, help me do it' version. Use that one.

beats.narration_text: Your move. The handoff prompt has Claude quiz you on whatever topic you're currently studying. It will ask you questions, not answer them — that's the whole game. Paste it in now.

props.command: Quiz me on [topic] — don't give me the answers. Ask one question at a time, wait for my response, then tell me if I'm right and why. After 3 questions, show me where my gaps are.

beats.narration_text: Claude, Not Your Answer. If the trade makes sense, subscribe. HAI. Liam, in for Bear.
